title: salvia carnosa subsp. argentea
intro: Salvia carnosa subsp. argentea, also known as Silver-leaved Sage, is a subspecies of Salvia carnosa, a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, with medicinal properties including its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Salvia carnosa subsp. argentea include its potential to aid in the treatment of bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats, as well as its use as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Herbal preparations of Salvia carnosa subsp. argentea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
